latestPlannerResponse.message: Here is the plan for Render client dashboard tiles.
latestPlannerResponse.featureUnderstanding: Add a new Dashboard screen at /dashboard for authenticated Client users. It renders exactly three tiles—New Brief, Screening Room, Brand Vault—in that order within a 4-up grid. Each tile includes a label and short descriptive subtext and navigates to: New Brief -> /new-brief, Screening Room -> /screening, Brand Vault -> /vault. No tiles are hidden or disabled for Client users in the PoC. No summary widgets appear. Standard header/nav and footer remain. Users belong to a single org; no org selector.

in: Create Dashboard UI screen at /dashboard for Client users
Render exactly three tiles labeled New Brief, Screening Room, Brand Vault
Maintain tile order: New Brief (1st), Screening Room (2nd), Brand Vault (3rd)
Include short descriptive subtext under each tile label (initial copy provided)
Use a 4-up grid layout (three tiles occupying available columns)
Exclude summary widgets from the Dashboard
Ensure keyboard accessibility and logical tab order
out: Feature work on destination pages beyond navigation
Role-based gating/feature flags beyond stated Client behavior

assumptions: Initial descriptive subtext will be refined later without layout changes.
4-up grid is four columns on desktop; responsive behavior follows existing design system.
Existing Rivvy Portal UI components/styles are reused; no new design tokens or icons needed.
Accessibility follows portal standards (focusable tiles, Enter/Space activation).
acceptanceCriteria: Given an authenticated Client user visits /dashboard, exactly three tiles are visible with labels: New Brief, Screening Room, Brand Vault.
Tiles render in the order: New Brief (first), Screening Room (second), Brand Vault (third) across supported viewports.
Each tile includes a visible descriptive subtext beneath its label.
Clicking New Brief navigates to /new-brief in the same tab.
Clicking Screening Room navigates to /screening in the same tab.
Clicking Brand Vault navigates to /vault in the same tab.
No tiles are hidden or disabled for Client users in the PoC.
No summary widgets (e.g., Recent Videos, Draft Briefs) appear on the Dashboard.
Tiles are keyboard-focusable and activate via Enter/Space; tab order matches visual order.
Standard header/nav and footer are present on the Dashboard.

implementationPlan.planTitle: Implementation Plan for Render client dashboard tiles

increments.title: Add Client Dashboard at /dashboard with three ordered tiles and subtext
increments.intent:
- UI architecture/model updates:
  - Add ui_screens entries:
    - Dashboard (/dashboard)
    - Screening Room (/screening)
  - Confirm existing ui_screens for New Brief (/new-brief) and Brand Vault (/vault) are unchanged.

- Frontend implementation (Rivvy Portal UI):
  - Routing:
    - Register a new route /dashboard rendering a DashboardPage component.
    - Ensure route is accessible to authenticated users; no special feature flags or conditional gating for Client users in PoC.
  - Screen component:
    - Create DashboardPage (e.g., src/screens/DashboardPage.tsx) that uses standard site layout (header/nav, footer) and excludes any summary widgets.
    - Layout: implement a 4-up grid using existing design system primitives (e.g., CSS grid: grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr); gap per design tokens). On smaller viewports, follow responsive defaults (e.g., 1–2 columns as defined by the system).
  - Tile component(s):
    - Reuse an existing Card/Tile component, or create a lightweight TileCard (src/components/TileCard.tsx) that accepts props: title, subtext, to (href), data-testid.
    - Render exactly three TileCard instances, in DOM order:
      1) New Brief -> title: "New Brief"; subtext: "Kick off a new project by submitting your brief."; to: "/new-brief"
      2) Screening Room -> title: "Screening Room"; subtext: "Watch and review your project videos with timecoded comments."; to: "/screening"
      3) Brand Vault -> title: "Brand Vault"; subtext: "Manage approved brand assets and versions."; to: "/vault"
    - Ensure each tile is a semantic link (<a> or Link) with proper accessible name (the title), includes subtext as descriptive text, and is keyboard-focusable. Activate on Enter/Space per standard Link/Button semantics (prefer Link).
  - Accessibility and order:
    - Visual order equals DOM order to ensure correct tab sequence: New Brief, Screening Room, Brand Vault.
    - Provide focus states consistent with the design system; ensure color contrast of text within tiles meets WCAG AA.
  - Navigation/visibility:
    - Do not implement any conditional visibility/disabled states for Client users in PoC.
    - Do not include any summary widgets (Recent Videos, Draft Briefs) on this page.

- Optional navigation integration:
  - If the global nav includes a Dashboard entry, point it to /dashboard; otherwise, this can be accessed via direct navigation without nav changes for PoC.

- Testing:
  - Unit/Component tests (React Testing Library):
    - Renders exactly three tiles and no more.
    - Verifies text content for labels and subtext of all three tiles.
    - Asserts tiles appear in correct DOM order.
    - Asserts hrefs: /new-brief, /screening, /vault.
    - Asserts absence of summary widgets container/components on DashboardPage.
  - Accessibility tests:
    - Run axe checks on DashboardPage; ensure no critical violations.
    - Verify tiles are focusable in order and activate via keyboard.
  - E2E (Cypress or equivalent):
    - Given an authenticated Client user, visit /dashboard; verify three tiles, click each tile and confirm navigation routes.

- Documentation:
  - Update UI route map to include /dashboard and /screening destinations.
  - Note PoC constraints: no conditional visibility, no widgets, single-org assumption.

- Release/rollout:
  - No feature flag; ship as default for Client users.
  - Monitor basic error logs; analytics out of scope.

messages.content: Welcome—let’s get started on “Render client dashboard tiles.” I have the product backlog and architecture context available to support this story. Are there any specific architecture entities or diagrams you’d like me to highlight as most relevant here?
I've provided the architectural context, please give me your understanding of this feature
Here’s my understanding; I have 7 questions to confirm details before we lock scope.

Answers to your questions:

Q: Should we create a new Dashboard UI screen at route /dashboard, and is that the exact path?
A: yes

Q: What is the exact route for the Screening Room tile (e.g., /screening-room or another path)?
A: These paths were provided in the architecture meta-model

Q: Should non-Client authenticated users (e.g., Producers/Admins) also see the same three tiles in the PoC, or is the Dashboard limited to Client users only?
A: the dashboard is different for a Producer.  Admin doesn't use dashboard

Q: Are standard header/nav and footer permitted on the Dashboard while still excluding summary widgets?
A: Ye

Q: Any specific layout requirements (e.g., 3-up grid on desktop, stacked on mobile) or tile dimensions/icons to follow?
A: 4-up grid

Q: Should tiles include any descriptive subtext, or label-only for the PoC?
A: descriptive subtext

Q: Do tiles require an organization context selector if a user belongs to multiple orgs, or is current default org context sufficient?
A: User is only single org
Thanks—updated with 4-up grid and subtext. I have 2 follow-ups before we lock scope.

Answers to your questions:

Q: Please confirm the exact Screening Room route (path string) to link the tile.
A: /screening

Q: Please provide the exact descriptive subtext copy for each tile (New Brief, Screening Room, Brand Vault).
A: You decide, I can refine wording after
Great—Dashboard at /dashboard with three ordered tiles and subtext, linking Screening Room to /screening. I’ll propose initial copy; ready to move to spec.
Okay, let's see if we need to break this work item into parts...
This item is small enough, so let's get the software architect to review this.
